records.specific_details: GAEC-2 Appeals
records.intended_results: To urge the Department of Agriculture, Food, and the Marine to address farmer concerns and move quickly to clarify the scope of GAEC-2 appeals.::That details of the GAEC-2 appeals process seem to infer that appeals are restricted to those who plan new drainage works in 2025, with signed declarations and evidence of planned drainage works required to accompany applications. This is very much at odds with what was briefed to IFA at both national and stakeholder meetings on this very emotive issue, and what has also been briefed to planners recently. It will cause consternation and annoy many farmers unless quickly clarified.::It was understood that soil samples; a planner's report; and geo-tagged photos may be required, but this is a curveball altogether. All farmers must be given the opportunity to appeal the GAEC-2 status of individual parcels, irrespective of whether lands were ever drained in the past or not, or whether new drainage is planned in 2025 or not.::That since indicative G

records.specific_details: Seeking Government measures to support the continued recovery of Ireland's tourism and hospitality industry
records.intended_results: Restoration of the 9% rate of VAT for hospitality food services::Measures to improve cost competitiveness and avoid any further cost increases::For a review to be carried out of options for assisting hotels to develop employer-led staff accommodation across the sector::Targeted funding for a national hotel retrofitting scheme to reduce carbon footprint::Increased investment and support for tourism marketing both domestically and overseas.::Insurance reform and policy to avoid any additional increases in insurance costs::Increased investment in training and skills development within the sector::Robust enforcement of regulations around short term lettings::Additional resources for tourism economic research::Avoid the introduction of a tourist tax::Increased air access including removal of cap at Dublin airport

records.specific_details: Dog Control & Sheep Worrying
records.intended_results: To highlight that sheep farmers are affected by the irresponsible behaviour of some dog owners more than any other sector.::To recognise that Comhairle na Tuaithe and its stakeholders (such as Leave no Trace and others) a lot of time and resources is being put into championing good behaviour, predominantly via Educational/Media campaigns, but unfortunately more is needed.::To highlight that some of the issues are because, politically and administratively responsibilities were scattered somewhat rather than falling under the remit of one body. This should improve with the Department of Agriculture taking up the reigns, but Dog Control must be fully resourced, with measures implemented in a timely manner and more importantly enforced.::To emphasise that lack of enforcement of obligations on dog owners and low levels of sanctions in this area are allowing this behaviour continue with sheep farmers having their flocks savaged by dogs on an almost daily basis at this point.::To highlight th

records.specific_details: IFA Farmer Health Check 2025
records.intended_results: To provide Farmers across the country with free health checks with the continued support of the Department of Agriculture, Food, and the Marine.::To build on the success of last year's rollout, the health checks include blood pressure monitoring, pulse and rhythm screening, BMI assessments, and lifestyle advice — all delivered by the expert nursing team from Croí.::To emphasise that the health of farmers is just as critical as the productivity of their land and livestock. IFA is proud to partner with Teagasc and Croí for this event to ensure that farmer health and wellbeing remain a priority. These checks are quick, confidential and could be lifesaving.

records.specific_details: Farmer Mental Health & Wellbeing
records.intended_results: To highlight that farming is more than an occupation in Ireland. It is a way of life that shapes our rural communities, our economy, and our national identity. But behind pride, tradition, and hard work, there is a growing and pressing concern  the mental health and wellbeing of our farmers.::That a recent study conducted by University College Dublin has laid bare the extent of this crisis. More than half of the farmers surveyed reported levels of depression ranging from moderate to extremely severe. Nearly 45 percent experienced significant anxiety, and over one-third reported high levels of stress.::Perhaps most worrying, almost one in four farmers surveyed was found to be at risk of suicide. These are not just statistics. They represent real people - our neighbours, our friends, our family members who are experiencing severe distress, often in silence.::To stress the importance of initiatives to raise awareness, train agricultural advisers in mental health literacy, and provide bett
lobbying_activities: Email
Meeting
Social Media

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

Seo thíos liosta de gach ionadaí poiblí agus eagras rialtais ó dheas lena raibh idirghníomhaíocht againn leo ar Twitter le ceithre mhí anuas@sealbhu@GaelLinn @Gaeloid@officialgaa@DeptRCDG@pleanteanga@UdarasnaG@Gaelcholaiste2@uniofgalway@meitheal@podonovan@AnGreasan @ForasnaGaeilge @jackfchambers@daracallery @OirnaGaeilge @OCallaghanJim@CatuIreland @CarrollJennifer@Feachtas_ie@ciaranahern@labour - Social Media

Ó am go chéile déanann Conradh na Gaeilge ionadaithe poiblí nó eagrais rialtais a chlibeáil i bpostálacha ar X (Twitter) ar ábhair a bhaineann ar bhealach amháin nó ar bhealach eile le pobal na Gaeilge agus Gaeltachta agus cur chun cinn na teanga in Éirinn agus níos faide i gcéin. Le ceithre mhí anuas, chuimsigh na hidirghníomhaíochtaí sin na téamaí a leanas:CEARTA: Agóid Náisiúnta na nGael Gaeilge ag an LGFA Tithíocht sa Ghaeltacht An Ghaeilge sa chóras oideachais - Social Media

records.specific_details: Kathleen O'Meara, Public Affairs Consultant and ILFA members made representations to John Lahart who supported the launch of the prospectus report in Leinster House AV Room on the 11th June 2025
records.intended_results: To secure a meeting with the Health Committee and Minister O'Neill for the request for a clinical care programme for patients living with pulmonary fibrosis in Ireland and to highlight unmet needs of those patients..

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine

lobbying_activities: Meeting took place in Leinster House AV Room for launch of prospectus report for ILFA. This was supported by John Lahart - Meeting

records.specific_details: Launch of Samaritans Ireland Impact Report and caller research – which included the following asks:
records.intended_results: •	Prioritise an urgent National Loneliness Strategy within the Taoiseach's office and establish national measurement. •	Mandate Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) to ensure content moderator wellbeing through transparent reporting, comprehensive mental health support standards (including contractors), and full inclusion under the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act.•	Commit to Samaritans training for all party staff to ensure responsible language on self-harm and suicide

Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth

records.intended_results: To invite Minister of State for Agriculture, Food, Fisheries and the Marine to address the IFA Kerry AGM.::To seek details on the response of the Department of Agriculture, Food, Fisheries and the Marine to the damage caused by Storm Eowyn.::To discuss the difficulties being experienced by the Horticulture Sector.

records.specific_details: Programme for Government
records.intended_results: Advocating for the inclusion of commitments to continue to support and expand the Patient Advocacy Service in the Programme for Government.
lobbying_activities: I emailed multiple TD's with personalised emails containing the same core asks for inclusion in the Programme for Government. Emails focused on securing continued support for the work of Patient Advocacy Service, including in long-term residential care settings, and to explore expanding PAS' remit to mental health advocacy. - Email

records.specific_details: Email to members of the Oireachtas concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from Forvis Mazars which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Email to members of the Oireachtas referring to the proposed implementation of the E-liquids tax using a self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp and highlighting the findings of an independent report from Forvis Mazars on the matter. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: The Irish Natura and Hill Farmers Association
records.specific_details: INHFA President and delegates met with Minister Martin Heydon to discuss concerns with the introduction of GAEC2
records.intended_results: To highlight concerns surrounding the implementation of GAEC2

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine

lobbying_activities: A day of protest was held outside AG House in March to highlight the concerns felt by members regarding the introduction of GAEC2 - Lobby day
Emails sent to arrange and finalise meetings - Email
Meeting held with representatives to discuss concerns - Meeting

records.specific_details: Activities relating to a briefing session at Leinster House on 25/02/25, where we held a session for all TDs and Senators to present the results of a survey on the experiences of hosts under the pledged accommodation project
records.intended_results: Support for our priorities in this policy area; raising awareness among elected representatives of host experiences
lobbying_activities: Event in Leinster House on 25/02/2025 - Event / Reception

records.lobbyist_name: Institute of Professional Auctioneers and Valuers
records.specific_details: Sought attendance, and briefed attendees at the informal IPAV Cross-Party Committee on property market reform.
records.intended_results: Support for the SLP Bill and legislative changes to streamline the residential purchasing process.
lobbying_activities: In person meeting with all. - Meeting
